howItWorks.intro: This black friday savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Dollar Savings = Original Price - Sale Price
Time Cost = (Drive + Wait Minutes / 60) x Hourly Wage
Real Savings = Dollar Savings - Shipping - Time Cost
Hourly Return = Real Savings / Total Hours Spent
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Are Black Friday deals actually good?
faq.answer: About 35% of Black Friday deals are genuine savings of 20%+ off the real regular price. Many retailers inflate prices before Black Friday to make discounts look larger. Use price tracking tools like CamelCamelCamel to verify.

faq.question: Should I factor in my time when evaluating deals?
faq.answer: Yes! If you spend 3 hours in lines and traffic to save $40, you effectively earned $13/hour — likely less than your regular wage. Online deals often provide similar savings without the time cost.

faq.question: When are the best deals actually available?
faq.answer: Cyber Monday often matches or beats Black Friday for electronics. The best TV deals are actually during Super Bowl week. Appliances are cheapest during holiday weekends (Memorial Day, Labor Day, Black Friday).

sections.title: What Is the Black Friday Savings Calculator?
sections.body: The Black Friday Savings Calculator helps you get precise numbers for your specific situation in seconds. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Making financial decisions without accurate numbers is like driving without a dashboard. This calculator gives you the specific figures for your situation so you can plan with confidence rather than rough estimates.
